input.command:
echo "--- pods ---"
kubectl get pods -n elasticsearch -o wide
echo "--- statefulsets ---"
kubectl get statefulset -n elasticsearch
echo "--- networkpolicies ---"
kubectl get networkpolicy -n elasticsearch -o yaml
echo "--- services ---"
kubectl get svc -n elasticsearch

apiVersion: v1
data:
  elasticsearch.yml: "cluster.name: es-cluster
node.name: 
node.roles: [ master,
    data, ingest ]
network.host: 0.0.0.0
discovery.seed_hosts:
  - es-cluster-0.es-cluster.default.svc.cluster.local

    \ - es-cluster-1.es-cluster.default.svc.cluster.local
  - es-cluster-2.es-cluster.default.svc.cluster.local
cluster.initial_master_nodes:

  - es-cluster-1
  - es-cluster-2
node.store.allow_mmap: false
xpack.security.enabled:
    false
xpack.security.http.ssl.enabled: false
xpack.security.transport.ssl.enabled:
    false
"

input.command:
echo "--- es-cluster-0 config file ---"
kubectl exec -n elasticsearch es-cluster-0 -- cat /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/elasticsearch.yml
echo "--- es-cluster-1 config file ---"
kubectl exec -n elasticsearch es-cluster-1 -- cat /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/elasticsearch.yml
echo "--- statefulset spec (env/command) ---"
kubectl get statefulset es-cluster -n elasticsearch -o yaml | sed -n '/containers:/,/volumes:/p'

content.content:
--- es-cluster-0 config file ---
cluster.name: es-cluster
node.name: ${POD_NAME}
node.roles: [ master, data, ingest ]
network.host: 0.0.0.0
discovery.seed_hosts:
  - es-cluster-0.es-cluster
  - es-cluster-1.es-cluster
  - es-cluster-2.es-cluster
cluster.initial_master_nodes:
  - es-cluster-0
  - es-cluster-1
  - es-cluster-2
node.store.allow_mmap: false
xpack.security.enabled: false
xpack.security.http.ssl.enabled: false
xpack.security.transport.ssl.enabled: false
--- es-cluster-1 config file ---
cluster.name: es-cluster
node.name: ${POD_NAME}
node.roles: [ master, data, ingest ]
network.host: 0.0.0.0
discovery.seed_hosts:
  - es-cluster-0.es-cluster
  - es-cluster-1.es-cluster
  - es-cluster-2.es-cluster
cluster.initial_master_nodes:
  - es-cluster-0
  - es-cluster-1
  - es-cluster-2
node.store.allow_mmap: false
xpack.security.enabled: false
xpack.security.http.ssl.enabled: false
xpack.security.transport.ssl.enabled: false
--- statefulset spec (env/command) ---
      containers:
      - env:
        - name: POD_NAME
          valueFrom:
            fieldRef:
              apiVersion: v1
              fieldPath: metadata.name
        - name: ES_JAVA_OPTS
          value: -Xms512m -Xmx512m
        image: docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:7.17.9
        imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
        name: elasticsearch
        ports:
        - containerPort: 9200
          name: http
          protocol: TCP
        - containerPort: 9300
          name: transport
          protocol: TCP
        readinessProbe:
          failureThreshold: 6
          initialDelaySeconds: 10
          periodSeconds: 5
          successThreshold: 1
          tcpSocket:
            port: 9200
          timeoutSeconds: 1
        resources:
          limits:
            cpu: "1"
            memory: 2Gi
          requests:
            cpu: 500m
            memory: 1Gi
        terminationMessagePath: /dev/termination-log
        terminationMessagePolicy: File
        volumeMounts:
        - mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/elasticsearch.yml
          name: es-config
          subPath: elasticsearch.yml
        - mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/data
          name: data
      dnsPolicy: ClusterFirst
      restartPolicy: Always
      schedulerName: default-scheduler
      securityContext: {}
      terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 30
      volumes:

content.text:
Found it: the `es-config` ConfigMap was corrupted — `node.name` is now empty (was `${POD_NAME}`) and `discovery.seed_hosts` points at the wrong namespace (`default.svc.cluster.local` instead of the actual `elasticsearch` namespace / short name). Pods 0 and 1 still run with the old correct config (subPath mounts don't live-update), but `es-cluster-2` restarted and picked up the broken config, so it crashes on startup.

I'll restore the ConfigMap to match the known-good config still running on pods 0/1.

input.content:
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: es-config
  namespace: elasticsearch
data:
  elasticsearch.yml: |
    cluster.name: es-cluster
    node.name: ${POD_NAME}
    node.roles: [ master, data, ingest ]
    network.host: 0.0.0.0
    discovery.seed_hosts:
      - es-cluster-0.es-cluster
      - es-cluster-1.es-cluster
      - es-cluster-2.es-cluster
    cluster.initial_master_nodes:
      - es-cluster-0
      - es-cluster-1
      - es-cluster-2
    node.store.allow_mmap: false
    xpack.security.enabled: false
    xpack.security.http.ssl.enabled: false
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.enabled: false

content.content: File created successfully at: /tmp/es-config-fixed.yaml (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

echo "--- diff check ---"
kubectl get configmap es-config -n elasticsearch -o jsonpath='{.data.elasticsearch\.yml}'

content.text: Now delete the crashing pod so it remounts the fixed ConfigMap (subPath mounts require a pod recreation to pick up changes).

input.command:
kubectl delete pod es-cluster-2 -n elasticsearch
kubectl rollout status statefulset/es-cluster -n elasticsearch --timeout=90s

description:
kubectl delete pod es-cluster-2 -n elasticsearch
kubectl rollout status statefulset/es-cluster -n elasticsearch --timeout=90s
